_evidence: C2 (commit 646c4e9a) live-smoke — provider-registry inventory sync / model-discovery against the local-rerank backend on :28417, using the BYOK-resolved user_model credential.
_path: GET /v1/model-registry/providers/019eb5a9-b8fb-7048-ab68-0a316e901126/models (gateway :3123 -> provider-registry :8208 -> listProviderInventory -> syncInventory -> adapter.ListModels(rerank_local => openaiAdapter) -> GET http://host.docker.internal:28417/v1/models)
_result: FAIL — discovery returned 0 items; the rerank model was NOT discovered/tagged.
_root_cause: Live local-rerank backend /v1/models shape is {"models":[{"id":"bge-reranker-v2-m3", "state":..., ...}]} — the model is keyed by `id` (no `name`, no `endpoints`). provider-registry's discovery routes the `models` key to parseCohereModels (adapters.go:686), but parseCohereModels reads m["name"] (adapters.go:707-710) and SKIPS any entry where name=="". The OpenAI parser only fires on a top-level `data` key, which this backend does not return. Net: no parser matches this real backend's id-keyed `models` array, so 0 models are discovered. openaiAdapter.ListModels swallows the mismatch (returns empty staticInventory), so the route 200s with an empty list rather than erroring.
_blocker_class: feature-code parser gap (NOT a missing credential / invalid key / unreachable endpoint — those are all valid; C3 verify proved a real round-trip with the same credential). The C2 parser was written against an assumed Cohere shape (name + endpoints) that this real backend does not emit. This is exactly the kind of contract drift a live-smoke surfaces vs unit/httptest fixtures.
_fix_direction_for_followup: Make parseCohereModels (or a new local-rerank-shape parser) accept `id` as a fallback when `name` is absent, OR add an id-keyed `{"models":[{"id":...}]}` branch to openaiAdapter.ListModels that classifies via classifyOpenAIModel(id) (which already maps a `*rerank*` id -> rerank). NOTE: this task is verification-only — feature code was NOT modified; tracked as a follow-up defect.
